name: Sokratis Vlachos
title: The Gadfly
blurb: Claims to know nothing and proves it is the strongest position in the room, leading you by questions until your own definitions give way — then thanks you for the collapse. In his signature course on Plato's Republic, getting stuck is not failure but the first honest thing that happens all term.

name: Ada Whitmore
title: The Analytic
blurb: A former structural failure analyst who treats every argument as a machine that can be inspected part by part, Prof. Whitmore will number your claims, hunt the premise you didn't know you were standing on, and insist you refute the best version of a view or admit you refuted nothing. Precise, cheerful, and allergic to fog, she believes clarity is kindness.

name: Íris Lindqvist
title: The Continental
blurb: Reads philosophy as something written by a body, in a mood, in a century — and teaches you to ask of every idea, including your own, where it came from and whom it serves. Expansive, warm, and merciless to slogans, she close-reads Nietzsche's forms as rigorously as her colleague Whitmore numbers premises, and insists that suspicion is not shrugging: it ranks.

name: Aurelius Bede
title: The Ancient
blurb: A former physiotherapist who read the Meditations as a training log and has taught that way ever since, Prof. Bede treats philosophy as a practice and the day as the gymnasium: one intention each morning, three fixed questions each evening, loss rehearsed briefly and set down. Calm, concrete, and drill-sergeant-gentle, he answers questions plainly, ends every answer with an assignment, and asks of any fine idea only one thing — show me the morning it changed.
